question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2023

Code: 56/2/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 35 questions.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains 35 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five Sections A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Questions no. 1 to 18 are multiple choice (MCQ) type questions, carrying 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Questions no. 19 to 25 very short answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Questions no. 26 to 30 are short answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Questions no. 31 and 32 are case-based questions carrying 4 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Questions no. 33 to 35 are long answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 2 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION - A
1.

Which of the following belongs to the class of Vinyl halides?

a. CH2 = CHCH2CH2Cl
b. {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_2=\underset{\underset{\mathrm{Br}}\vert}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}
c. CH2 = CH - CH2 - Br
d. CH {tex}\equiv{/tex} C - Br
1
2.

What is the secondary valency of Cobalt in [Co(en2)Cl2]+?

a. 6
b. 4
c. 2
d. 8
1
3.

When Benzene diazonium chloride reacts with phenol, it forms a dye. This reaction is called

a. Diazotisation reaction
b. Condensation reaction
c. Coupling reaction
d. Acetylation reaction
1
4.

The slope in the plot of [R] vs. time for a zero order reaction is

a. {tex}\frac{+\mathrm{k}}{2.303}{/tex}
b. -k
c. {tex}\frac{-\mathrm{k}}{2.303}{/tex}
d. +k
1
5.

Proteins are polymers of

a. Nucleic acids
b. Amino acids
c. Monosaccharides
d. Amines
1
6.

Retention of configuration is observed in

a. SN1 reaction
b. SN2 reaction
c. Neither SN1 nor SN2 reaction
d. SN2 reaction as well as SN1 reaction
1
7.

An azeotropic mixture of two liquids will have a boiling point lower than either of the two liquids when it

a. shows a negative deviation from Raoult’s law
b. forms an ideal solution
c. shows a positive deviation from Raoult’s law
d. is saturated
1
8.

Which of the following solutions of KCl will have the highest value of molar conductivity?

a. 0.01 M
b. 1 M
c. 0.5 M
d. 0.1 M
1
9.

Which of the following does not give Cannizaro reaction?

a. (CH3)3 C - CHO
b. (CH3)2 CH - CHO
c.
d. HCHO
1
10.

Which of the following reactions are feasible?

a. CH3CH2Br + Na+ O-C(CH3)3 {tex}\to{/tex} CH3CH2 - O - C (CH3)3
b. (CH3)3 C - Cl + Na+ O-CH2CH3 {tex}\to{/tex} CH3CH2 - O - C(CH3)3
c. Both (a) and (b)
d. Neither (a) nor (b)
1
11.

Aldehydes and ketones react with hydroxylamine to form

a. hydrazones
b. Oxime
c. semicarbazones
d. cyanohydrins
1
12.

For a reaction 2A {tex}\to{/tex} 3B, rate of reaction {tex}-\frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{A}]}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} is equal to

a. {tex}\frac{+3}{2} \frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{B}]}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{+2}{3} \frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{B}]}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{+1}{3} \frac{\mathrm{d}[\mathrm{B}]}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}
d. {tex}+\frac{2 \mathrm{~d}[\mathrm{~B}]}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}
1
13.

The formula of the complex dichloridobis (ethane-1, 2-diamine) platinum (IV) nitrate is

a. [Pt Cl2(en)2 (NO3)2]
b. [Pt Cl2(en)2] (NO3)2
c. [Pt Cl2(en)2 (NO3)] NO3
d. [Pt (en)2 (NO3)2] Cl2
1
14.

Which one among the following metals of 3d series has the lowest melting point?

a. Fe
b. Mn
c. Zn
d. Cu
1

Given below are two statements labelled as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Select the most appropriate answer from the options given below :

  1. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
  2. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
  3. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
  4. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
15.

Assertion (A): Elevation in boiling point is a colligative property.
Reason (R): The lowering of vapour pressure of solution causes elevation in boiling point.

a. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
b. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
c. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
d. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
1
16.

Assertion (A): Chlorobenzene is resistant to electrophilic substitution reaction.
Reason (R): C-Cl bond in chlorobenzene acquires partial double bond characters due to resonance.

a. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
b. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
c. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
d. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
1
17.

Assertion (A): Conductivity decreases with decrease in concentration of electrolyte.
Reason (R): Number of ions per unit volume that carry the current in a solution decreases on dilution.

a. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
b. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
c. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
d. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
1
18.

Assertion (A): Transition metals have high enthalpy of atomisation.
Reason (R): Greater number of unpaired electrons in transition metals results in weak metallic bonding.

a. Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
b. Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
c. (A) is true, but (R) is false.
d. (A) is false, but (R) is true.
1

SECTION - B
19.(i)

Give reasons:

  1. Mercury cell delivers a constant potential during its life time.
  2. In the experimental determination of electrolytic conductance, Direct Current (DC) is not used.
 2

OR
(ii)

Define fuel cell with an example. What advantages do the fuel cells have over primary and secondary batteries?

 2
20.

The vapour pressure of pure liquid X and pure liquid Y at 25oC are 120 mm Hg and 160 mm Hg respectively. If equal moles of X and Y are mixed to form an ideal solution, calculate the vapour pressure of the solution.

 2
21.(i)

Write the IUPAC names of the following:

  1. [Co(NH3)5(ONO)]2+
  2. K2[NiCl4]
 2

OR
(ii)
  1. What is a chelate complex? Give one example.
  2. What are heteroleptic complexes? Give one example.
 2
22.
  1. The conversion of molecule A to B followed second order kinetics. If concentration of A increased to three times, how will it affect the rate of formation of B?
  2. Define Pseudo first order reaction with an example.
 2
23.
  1. What is the difference between a nucleoside and nucleotide?
  2. What products would be formed when a nucleotide from DNA containing thymine is hydrolysed?
 2
24.

Write the chemical equation involved in the following:

  1. Kolbe’s reaction
  2. Williamson synthesis
 2
25.

Do the following conversions in not more than two steps:

  1. CH3CN to {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{\underset{\mathrm O}{\vert\vert}}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}
 2

SECTION C
26.
  1. Draw the geometrical isomers of [Co(en)2Cl2]2+. Which geometrical isomer of [Co(en)2Cl2]2+ is not optically active and why?
  2. Write the hybridisation and magnetic behaviour of [CoF6]3-.
    [Given: Atomic number of Co = 27]
 3
27.

When 19.5 g of F - CH2 – COOH (Molar mass = 78 g mol-1), is dissolved in 500 g of water, the depression in freezing point is observed to be 1oC.
Calculate the degree of dissociation of F - CH2 - COOH.
[Given: Kf for water = 1.86 K kg mol-1]

 3
28.

Answer any 3 of the following:

  1. Which isomer of C5H10 gives a single monochloro compound C5H9Cl in bright sunlight?
  2. Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of reactivity towards SN2 reaction:
    2-Bromopentane, 1-Bromopentane, 2-Bromo-2-methylbutane
  3. Why p-dichlorobenzene has higher melting point than those of ortho-and meta-isomers?
  4. Identify A and B in the following:
 3
29.

A first order reaction is 50% complete in 30 minutes at 300 K and in 10 minutes at 320 K. Calculate activation energy (Ea) for the reaction. [R = 8.314 J K-1 mol-1]
[Given: log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771, log 4 = 0.6021]

 3
30.(i)
  1. Write the mechanism of the following reaction:
    2CH3CH2OH{tex} \underset{413 \mathrm{~K}}{\stackrel{\mathrm{H}^{+}}{\longrightarrow}}{/tex} CH3 - CH2 - O - CH2 - CH3 + H2O
  2. Why ortho-nitrophenol is steam volatile while para-nitrophenol is not?
 3

OR
(ii)

What happens when

  1. Anisole is treated with CH3Cl/anhydrous AlCl3?
  2. Phenol is oxidised with Na2Cr2O7/H+?
  3. (CH3)3C - OH is heated with Cu/573 K?

Write chemical equation in support of your answer.

 3

SECTION - D

The following questions are case based questions. Read the passage carefully and answer the questions that follow :
31.

Carbohydrates are optically active polyhydroxy aldehydes and ketones. They are also called saccharides. All those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution and Tollen’s reagent are referred to as reducing sugars. Glucose, the most important source of energy for mammals, is obtained by the hydrolysis of starch. Vitamins are accessory food factors required in the diet. Proteins are the polymers of {tex}\alpha{/tex}-amino acids and perform various structural and dynamic functions in the organisms. Deficiency of vitamins leads to many diseases.
(i)

The penta-acetate of glucose does not react with Hydroxylamine. What does it indicate?

 1
(ii)

Why cannot vitamin C be stored in our body?

 1
(iii)

Define the following as related to proteins:

  1. Peptide linkage
  2. Denaturation
 2

OR
(iii)

Define the following as related to carbohydrates:

  1. Anomers
  2. Glycosidic linkage
 2
32.

The carbon – oxygen double bond is polarised in aldehydes and ketones due to higher electronegativity of oxygen relative to carbon. Therefore they undergo nucleophilic addition reactions with a number of nucleophiles such as HCN, NaHSO3, alcohols, ammonia derivatives and Grignard reagents. Aldehydes are easily oxidised by mild oxidising agents as compared to ketones. The carbonyl group of carboxylic acid does not give reactions of aldehydes and ketones. Carboxylic acids are considerably more acidic than alcohols and most of simple phenols.
(i)

Write the name of the product when an aldehyde reacts with excess alcohol in presence of dry HCl.

 1
(ii)

Why carboxylic acid is a stronger acid than phenol?

 1
(iii)
  1. Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of their reactivity towards CH3MgBr:
    {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3\mathrm{CHO},{({\mathrm{CH}}_3)}_3\mathrm C-\underset{\underset{\mathrm O}{\vert\vert}}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3,\;{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{\underset{\mathrm O}{\vert\vert}}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}
  2. Write a chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone.
 2

OR
(iii)

Write the main product in the following:

 2

SECTION - E
33.
  1. Conductivity of 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 M methanoic acid is 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 S cm-1. Calculate its molar conductivity and degree of dissociation if {tex}\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}^0{/tex} for methanoic acid is 404 S cm2mol-1.
  2. Calculate the {tex}\Delta_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{G}^{\circ}{/tex} and log Kc for the given reaction at 298 K:
    {tex}\mathrm{Ni}_{(\mathrm{s})}+2 \mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{aq})}^{+} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}{ }_{(\mathrm{aq})}+2 \mathrm{Ag}_{(\mathrm{s})}{/tex}
    Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{i}}}^{\circ}{ }^{2+} / \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{i}}=-0.25 \mathrm{~V}, \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ag}^{+} / \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}=+0.80 \mathrm{~V}{/tex}
    1 F = 96500 C mol-1.
 5
34.(i)

Account for the following:

    1. Eo value for Mn3+/Mn2+ couple is much more positive than that for Cr3+/Cr2+.
    2. Sc3+ is colourless whereas Ti3+ is coloured in an aqueous solution.
    3. Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states.
  2. Write the chemical equations for the preparation of KMnO4 from MnO2.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Account for the following:
    1. Transition metals form alloys.
    2. Ce4+ is a strong oxidising agent.
  2. Write one similarity and one difference between chemistry of Lanthanoids and Actinoids.
  3. Complete the following ionic equation:
    {tex}\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-} \longrightarrow{/tex}
 5
35.(i)
  1. Give reasons:
    1. Aniline on nitration gives good amount of m-nitroaniline, though -NH2 group is o/p directing in electrophilic substitution reactions.
    2. (CH3)2 NH is more basic than (CH3)3N in an aqueous solution.
    3. Ammonolysis of alkyl halides is not a good method to prepare pure primary amines.
  2. Write the reaction involved in the following:
    1. Carbyl amine test
    2. Gabriel phthalimide synthesis
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Write the structures of A, B and C in the following reactions:
  2. Why aniline does not undergo Friedal-Crafts reaction?
  3. Arrange the following in increasing order of their boiling point:
    C2H5OH, C2H5NH2, (C2H5)3N
 5

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains 35 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five Sections A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Questions no. 1 to 18 are multiple choice (MCQ) type questions, carrying 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Questions no. 19 to 25 very short answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Questions no. 26 to 30 are short answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Questions no. 31 and 32 are case-based questions carrying 4 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Questions no. 33 to 35 are long answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 2 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION : A
1.

Which of the following belongs to the class of Vinyl halides?

a. CH2 = CHCH2CH2Cl
b. {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_2=\underset{\underset{\mathrm{Br}}\vert}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}
c. CH2 = CH - CH2 - Br
d. CH {tex}\equiv{/tex} C - Br
Correct Answer: (b) {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_2=\underset{\underset{\mathrm{Br}}\vert}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}
Explanation:

{tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_2=\underset{\underset{\mathrm{Br}}\vert}{\mathrm C}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3{/tex}belongs to vinyl halides

What is the secondary valency of Cobalt in [Co(en2)Cl2]+?

a. 6
b. 4
c. 2
d. 8
Correct Answer: (a) 6
Explanation:

The oxidation state of cobalt is +3, and hence, the primary valency will be 3. The secondary valency is 6, as there are 4 NH3 molecules and 2 Cl- ions linked to the cobalt atom.

Retention of configuration is observed in

a. SN1 reaction
b. SN2 reaction
c. Neither SN1 nor SN2 reaction
d. SN2 reaction as well as SN1 reaction
Correct Answer: (a) SN1 reaction
Explanation:

Retention of configuration is observed in SN1 reaction. SN1 reactions proceed through a carbocation intermediate, which allows for a nucleophile to attack from either side, potentially leading to both retention and inversion of configuration, resulting in a racemic mixture.

When 19.5 g of F - CH2 – COOH (Molar mass = 78 g mol-1), is dissolved in 500 g of water, the depression in freezing point is observed to be 1oC.
Calculate the degree of dissociation of F - CH2 - COOH.
[Given: Kf for water = 1.86 K kg mol-1]

Solution:

{tex}\Delta \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{f}}=\mathrm{i} \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}} \mathrm{m}{/tex}
{tex}=\mathrm{iK}_{\mathrm{f}} \frac{\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B}}}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{B}}} \times \frac{1000}{\mathrm{~W}_{\mathrm{A}}}{/tex}
{tex}1=\mathrm{i} \times 1.86 \times \frac{19.5}{78} \times \frac{1000}{500}{/tex}
i = 1.075
{tex}\alpha=\frac{\mathrm{i}-1}{\mathrm{n}-1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-075-1}{2-1}{/tex}

Degree of dissociation= 0.075

A first order reaction is 50% complete in 30 minutes at 300 K and in 10 minutes at 320 K. Calculate activation energy (Ea) for the reaction. [R = 8.314 J K-1 mol-1]
[Given: log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771, log 4 = 0.6021]

Solution:

{tex}k=\frac{0.693}{t}{/tex}
{tex}k_1=\frac{0.693}{30}{/tex} min-1
{tex}k_2=\frac{0.693}{10}{/tex} min-1
log{tex}\frac{\mathbf{k}_2}{\mathrm{k}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{Ea}}{2 \cdot 303 \mathrm{R}}\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{~T}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}\right]{/tex}
log 3 = {tex}\frac{\text { Ea }}{2.303 \times 8.314}\left[\frac{1}{300}-\frac{1}{320}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{E_a}=\frac{0.4771 \times 19 \cdot 147 \times 300 \times 320}{20}{/tex}
Activation energy = Ea = 43848 J mol-1 or 43.848 KJ mol-1 or 43.85 k J mol-1
